questions.questionText:
For each of the following, write down whether the data is **discrete** or **continuous**.
(a) The number of books on a shelf.
(b) The mass of a puppy.
(c) The number of goals scored in a match.
(d) The time taken to run 100 metres.
workingSteps: (a) Books can only be counted in whole numbers → discrete
(b) Mass can take any value → continuous
(c) Goals are counted in whole numbers → discrete
(d) Time can take any value → continuous
questions.explanation: Discrete data can only take specific values (usually whole numbers). Continuous data can take any value within a range.

questions.questionText:
Write down whether each variable is **qualitative** (categorical) or **quantitative** (numerical).
(a) The colour of a car.
(b) The height of a student.
workingSteps: (a) Colour describes a category, not a number → qualitative
(b) Height is measured as a number → quantitative
questions.explanation: Qualitative data is descriptive (categories). Quantitative data is numerical.

questions.context:
A class of 30 students was asked about their favourite subject. The results are shown below.

| Subject | Frequency |
|---------|----------|
| Maths | 9 |
| English | 7 |
| Science | 8 |
| Art | 4 |
| PE | 2 |

parts.label: a
workingSteps: The highest frequency is 9, which corresponds to Maths.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText:
Find the relative frequency of students who chose Science.
Give your answer as a decimal.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Explain why subject is qualitative data.
parts.correctAnswer: Subject names are categories, not numbers

markScheme.desc: Subject names are categories / cannot be measured numerically oe

parts.label: d
parts.questionText:
A student says: 'The frequency data in this table is discrete.' 
Do you agree? Give a reason for your answer.
parts.correctAnswer: Yes — frequencies are counted in whole numbers so they are discrete

markScheme.desc: Frequencies are whole number counts / cannot take fractional values oe

parts.label: e
parts.questionText:
Another student records the time (in minutes) each student spends on homework each evening.
Write down the type of data this represents.
workingSteps: Time can take any value → continuous
